supplier_name: University Hospitals of Leicester NHS Trust
one_line_value_proposition: NICE HTG764-recommended web-based cardiac rehabilitation platform with direct RCT evidence of secondary CV event risk reduction — developed by NHS clinicians and reaching patients who would not attend conventional CR.
why_it_matters_locally: Cardiac rehabilitation uptake remains well below NHS targets, with many patients unable or unwilling to attend face-to-face sessions. Activate Your Heart is part of the i-IMPACT platform at UHL and demonstrated that 90% of pilot patients would not have attended conventional CR, showing the platform reaches an otherwise unreachable population.
target_problem_statement: Low cardiac rehabilitation uptake, limited face-to-face CR capacity, and inability to reach patients who decline or cannot attend centre-based programmes.

nice_guidance_refs.note: Conditionally recommended — digital platforms to support cardiac rehabilitation

evidence_summary: Two RCTs evaluating exercise capacity, cardiovascular risk factors and health-related quality of life. One of four HTG764 platforms with direct clinical evidence suggesting potential reduction in secondary CV event risk. Pilot of 68 patients showed 90% would not have attended conventional CR. NICE EAG economic model found the platform cost-saving vs conventional rehabilitation.

case_studies.title: Health Foundation Shine programme pilot
case_studies.setting: University Hospitals of Leicester NHS Trust
case_studies.outcome: 68 patients completed the programme. 90% reported they would not have attended conventional CR. 54% stated they would not have attended any other form of rehabilitation. Staff confidence enhanced and freed time for patients with complex needs.
case_studies.caveat: Pilot study — not a peer-reviewed RCT. Outcomes reflect specific local deployment conditions.
case_studies.source: Health Foundation Shine programme case study

live_sites: University Hospitals of Leicester NHS Trust, Leicestershire
patients_covered_note: Approximately 2,000 patients/year referred through the Leicester CR service overall. Platform-specific user numbers not publicly available.
dtac_note: Did not submit for central DTAC assessment (pre-submission stage). DTAC approval is a condition of the NICE EVA recommendation and must be obtained before commissioning.
dspt_status: Operates within UHL NHS Trust IG framework
cyber_notes: NHS Trust-developed product — operates within UHL information governance framework.
gdpr_note: NHS Trust-hosted product. Operates within UHL NHS Trust IG and data governance framework.
target_patients: Adults with CVD unable or unwilling to attend face-to-face cardiac rehabilitation sessions.
exclusions: Clinical assessment required before offering digital CR. Not suitable as sole provision for patients requiring supervised exercise.
onboarding_detail: Patients assessed face-to-face then offered digital delivery as part of a 'menu of choice.' One-stop-shop onboarding appointment loads the personalised plan. Early follow-up calls (e.g. at 2 weeks) to prevent drop-off.
supplier_wrap: Web-based platform hosted by UHL NHS Trust. 'Ask the Expert' feature for asynchronous patient-clinician communication. Traffic light system flags worsening symptoms or disengagement.
local_wraparound_detail: Initial face-to-face clinical assessment by a trained CR professional required. Staff training on the platform and menu-of-choice model needed. If a patient struggles with technology, they can switch back to in-person care.
training_note: Staff training on the platform and menu-of-choice model required.
monitoring_model: Clinician dashboards with traffic light system (Red/Amber/Green) flag worsening symptoms or disengagement. Asynchronous communication via 'Ask the Expert' feature.
monitoring_note: Not a continuously monitored service. Clinicians review patient-entered data (symptoms, exertion scores, step counts) via dashboard. Traffic light alerts flag deterioration.
escalation_note: Patients can switch to in-person care if they struggle with the digital format. Worsening symptoms flagged by traffic light system require clinical follow-up.
operating_hours_caveat: Not a monitored emergency service. Patients must have access to standard NHS escalation routes.
implementation_prerequisites: Smartphone, tablet, or computer with internet access
Initial face-to-face clinical assessment by trained CR professional
Staff training on the platform and menu-of-choice model
indicative_price_text: NHS Trust–developed product — no public national tariff. When benchmarked against similar hybrid cardiac rehabilitation digital programmes, all-in indicative costs are often discussed in the £40–£80 per completed patient range depending on cohort and integration; confirm with University Hospitals of Leicester NHS Trust.
service_wrap_description: NHS Trust–developed platform. Implementation support, hosting and clinical dashboard scope are agreed locally with UHL — not a standard commercial SaaS wrap.
service_wrap_note: NHS Trust-developed platform. Implementation support model not publicly described.
contract_note: Information not available. NICE advises that contract negotiations should consider the 3-year evidence generation period.
expected_benefit_note: NICE EAG economic model found Activate Your Heart cost-saving vs conventional rehabilitation, with small quality-of-life gains. Savings arose mainly from reduced delivery costs between initial and final in-person sessions.
tariff_considerations: Cardiac rehabilitation delivery is typically commissioned rather than separately tariffed per session in many systems. Model using CR service costs. Platform may reduce the cost of delivering CR by replacing some face-to-face sessions with digital delivery.
provider_income_note: If hybrid CR reduces centre-based contacts, trusts may see shifts in activity and income versus traditional group CR — align gainshare or pathway budgets between commissioner and provider.
roi_note: NICE EAG model found cost-saving vs conventional rehabilitation. Reaches patients who would not otherwise attend CR, expanding the total patient pool.
minimum_conditions_for_success: Dedicated CR clinical team, face-to-face assessment capacity for initial patient evaluation, staff trained on platform use.
procurement_notes: Information not available. NHS Trust-developed product — confirm procurement route with UHL.

technical_integrations.device_integration: No specific device integration described. Web-based platform tracks patient-entered symptoms, exertion scores and step counts.

context_of_use.population: Adults with CVD — coronary heart disease, bypass/valve surgery rehab, spontaneous coronary artery dissection, heart failure, arrhythmias
context_of_use.therapeutic_purpose: Structured home-based cardiac rehabilitation with personalised exercise plans, education and clinician-monitored symptom tracking
context_of_use.hcp_involvement: Active — initial face-to-face assessment required, ongoing dashboard monitoring, Ask the Expert communication
context_of_use.nice_scope: NICE HTG764 — conditionally recommended during 3-year evidence generation period

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: Activate Your Heart RCT — exercise capacity and CV risk factors
clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: University Hospitals of Leicester NHS Trust / Coventry University
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: RCT evaluating exercise capacity, cardiovascular risk factors and health-related quality of life. One of four HTG764 platforms with direct clinical evidence suggesting potential reduction in secondary CV event risk.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Publication details not publicly confirmed. Verify with UHL for full citation.
clinical_evidence_detailed.data_quality_note: Referenced in NICE HTG764 assessment but full publication details not available in the dossier.

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: Activate Your Heart RCT — second trial
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Second RCT evaluating exercise capacity, cardiovascular risk factors and health-related quality of life outcomes for the Activate Your Heart platform.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Publication details not publicly confirmed. Verify with UHL for full citation.
clinical_evidence_detailed.data_quality_note: Referenced in NICE HTG764 assessment but full publication details not available in the dossier.

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: Health Foundation Shine programme — Activate Your Heart pilot
clinical_evidence_detailed.authors: Singh S, Boyce S et al.
clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: University Hospitals of Leicester NHS Trust
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: 68 patients completed the programme. 90% reported they would not have attended conventional CR. 54% stated they would not have attended any other form of rehabilitation. Staff confidence enhanced and freed time for patients with complex needs.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Pilot study — not a peer-reviewed RCT. Outcomes reflect specific local deployment conditions.

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: NICE EVA HTG764 — Digital platforms to support cardiac rehabilitation
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Conditionally recommended. One of four platforms with direct clinical evidence suggesting potential reduction in secondary CV event risk. EAG economic model found Activate Your Heart cost-saving vs conventional rehabilitation with small quality-of-life gains. Savings arose mainly from reduced delivery costs between initial and final in-person sessions.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Conditional recommendation — 3-year evidence generation period required. DTAC approval must be obtained.

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: UHL Cardiac Rehabilitation Service — i-IMPACT programme
clinical_evidence_detailed.authors: University Hospitals of Leicester NHS Trust
clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: University Hospitals of Leicester NHS Trust, Leicestershire
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Approximately 2,000 patients/year referred through the Leicester CR service overall. Platform operates as part of the i-IMPACT web-based rehabilitation programme. Developed by Professor Sally Singh and Modern Matron Sally Boyce with NHS Improvement Heart team support.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Institutional source — describes service model rather than clinical outcomes.

demo_notes: Contact University Hospitals of Leicester for further information.
source_summary: NICE HTG764, Health Foundation Shine programme case study, activateyourheart.org.uk.
confidence_note: Content based on NICE guidance and publicly available information. DTAC approval must be obtained before commissioning. Verify all details with UHL before procurement.
